ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON DEC-07-2006 IN 206, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREF TRIAL SETTING PURSUANT TO CCP 36(B) WAS GRANTED. THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 4/2/07 @ 11:15AM IN DEPT. 206; SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE SET FOR 3/23/07 @ 9:30AM IN DEPT. 304. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL THE CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULED TO. ALL PLAINTIFF HEIRS' DEPOSITIONS TO BE SCHEDULED BY 1/12/07. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE ALL PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND CAUSATION WITNESSES AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 1/11/07, AND SHALL ALSO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS THEY INTEND TO USE AT TRIAL AS TO PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY THAT DATE. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. DEFENDANTS TO PROVIDE INFORMATION REGARDING PROPOSITION 51 WITNESSES AND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE WITNESSES BY 2/1/07. IF DEFENDANTS LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. NO LATER THAN 12 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, EACH SIDE MAY IDENTIFY UP TO THREE EXPERT WITNESSES PERCEIVED TO BE NECESSARY TO BE DEPOSED FOR MEANINGFUL SETTLEMENT DISCUSSIONS TO OCCUR. THESE EXPERTS WILL BE DEPOSED PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE. TRIAL WILL NOT BE DELAYED FOR THE PURPOSE OF DEPOSING EXPERTS. PLAINTIFF TO DISCLOSE BANKRUPTCY TRUST INFORMATION BY 1/12/07. STANDARD INTERROGATORY RESPONSES TO BE PROVIDED BY 1/8/07. AUTHORIZATIONS OF CHARLES' FATHER TO BE PROVIDED WITHIN 30 DAYS FROM THIS DATE. WRITTEN ORDER PENDING. JUDGE ROBERT DONDERO; CLERK: V. MU; REPORTER: J. BRYCE, CSR #3321 (206)

Off Calendar May-15-2008 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on Petition to Approve a Compromise of a Disputed Claim of a Minor.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Jennifer K. Berg, Esq. appeared with petitioner Patsy Lee Piazza who was present as Guardian ad litem for the minor, Kenneth Piazza, who was also present at today's hearing.
Petitioner was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant Advocate Mines, Ltd.

On the basis of evidence received, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved.

Total Settlement - $51,557.03.
Attorney's Fees - $11,813.39.
Medical Expenses - $0.00.
Costs - $4,303.46.

A net balance of $35,440.18 shall be deposited into a blocked account with Bank of America, located at 200 The Pruneyard, Campbell, CA 95008. No withdrawals shall be made without a written Court Order or until the minor reaches the age of 18 years.

Orders Approving Compromise of Claim, and to to Deposit Money Into Blocked Account, were submitted to and signed by the Court.
